page: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v3 is a dedicated virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz. The server is configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs, providing a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. It includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core density of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities paired with constrained multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score of 1897.68 and Geekbench single-core score of 1660.0 both fall into the average tier. However, multi-core execution is limited, as evidenced by a weak multi-core stress-ng score of 3765.27 and a poor multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 19.65 GB/sec. Memory performance is mixed; while the Passmark memory score is average, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are weak at 27.83 GB/sec and 19.41 GB/sec respectively.

Given its hardware profile, the DC2s_v3 offers a qualitative balance of high memory density relative to its low vCPU count, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring secure, memory-heavy execution without intensive parallel processing. It is well-suited for lightweight database operations, basic text processing, and small-scale language model inference, where its 135M model prompt processing speed of 420.8 tokens/sec provides average performance. It is less suitable for heavily multithreaded tasks, compilation, or high-throughput web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v3 is a dedicated x86_64 virtual machine within the DCSv3 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, delivering 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs. The instance features 16.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, though it lacks bundled local storage and GPU resources. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1660.0, but weak multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-sensitive, low-concurrency workloads. It is best suited for lightweight database tasks, text processing, and small-scale LLM inference, while being less optimal for compute-intensive parallel processing or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC2s_v3 is a dedicated virtual machine featuring 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering average single-core performance for memory-dense, low-concurrency workloads.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core x86_64 virtual machine offering high memory density for secure, low-concurrency workloads and basic text processing.
